title: Schmaltzia galeottii
taxonomy: Schmaltzia galeottii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae. This family includes various plants such as cashews and poison ivy. Schmaltzia galeottii is native to Mexico and is a member of the genus Schmaltzia, which is a group of plants often referred to as Mexican cashews. The Anacardiaceae family is part of the larger order Sapindales, which consists of plants with similar characteristics such as fruit and flower arrangements. Understanding the classification of Schmaltzia galeottii provides insight into the plant's evolutionary history and relationships with other organisms.
